item.title: This Is Why We Need a Ballroom
item.description: Many historical disasters would have been prevented if we had one of these in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: Atlantic Reads:Screen PeopleWith Megan Garber
item.description: Staff writer Megan Garber and Adrienne LaFrance,The Atlantic’s executive editor, discuss Garber’s new book,Screen People: How We Entertained Ourselves Into a State of Emergency.

item.title: Calling Trump a Tyrant Is Not a Call to Violence
item.description: Conservatives want to police how we talk about Trump—while excusing how the president talks about everyone else.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: A Land of Artists
item.description: Lee Friedlander’s view of America
item.dc:creator: Hua Hsu

item.title: The Avant-Garde Path to God
item.description: A new book explores how contemporary art can offer glimpses of the divine.
item.dc:creator: Luis Parrales

item.title: Anthropic’s Little Brother
item.description: OpenAI is racing to catch up to its greatest rival.
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: The Lost Idealism of Heartland Rock
item.description: The genre of Bob Seger and John Mellencamp reached across the ideological spectrum in a way that seems unimaginable today.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hamilton

item.title: A Mediocre Public-School Education for Just $40,000 a Pupil
item.description: How New York City’s education budget became an untouchable money pit
item.dc:creator: Marc Novicoff

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Asian Tigers, Arctic Mammals
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Americans Once Understood Birthright Citizenship
item.description: Newspaper columnists instructed generations of citizens about the Fourteenth Amendment. Today, the country seems to have forgotten how clear the law is.
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Glickman

item.title: So Nobody Is Going to Pay Taxes Now?
item.description: America actually needs a tax base.
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: Is It the Shoes?
item.description: The marathon’s impossible barrier was broken.
item.dc:creator: Alex Hutchinson

item.title: MAHA’s Perfect Villain
item.description: Glyphosate highlights the movement’s horseshoe politics and has nothing to do with vaccines.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Walecki

item.title: The Shooting Is Not a Reason to Speedrun Trump’s Ballroom
item.description: The safe outcome on Saturday makes the case for deliberation and care.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Even Hollywood’s Funniest People Have to Compromise
item.description: One of comedy’s best leading men returns to cinemas with … an action thriller?
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: What the Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Means for the World Cup, America’s 250th, and Other Major Events
item.description: Some level of risk is inevitable in a free society.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Kayyem

item.title: What We Learn About Trump in His Rare Moments of Self-Reflection
item.description: For a brief moment this weekend, the president appeared introspective.
item.dc:creator: Matt Viser

item.title: Winners of the GDT Nature Photographer of the Year 2026
item.description: A collection of some of this year’s winning and honored photographs. Nearly 9,000 entries were submitted to organizers by member photographers from 15 countries.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: The Pentagon May Not Be Telling Trump the Full Picture About the War
item.description: Vice President Vance is worried that the U.S. is running low on weapons.
item.dc:creator: Missy Ryan

item.title: The American Pope vs. the American President
item.description: The pontiff has proved unwilling to subordinate his faith to politics, or to adjust his commitment to the Gospel in exchange for access to power.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wehner
